title: Should Shinji Have Kept The DSS Choker
display_title: Should Shinji Have Kept The DSS Choker
wikitext:
[]: Should Shinji Have Kept The DSS Choker?             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Apr 04, 2014 2:24 am Okay. Obviously not! Anno wouldn't do that! He's the main character so until Final, the narrative is obviously going to favor what keeps him alive. Not to mention Kaworu did it to earn his trust and the poor kid was so racked with guilt and sadness and insomnia he couldn't exactly reason or think it through properly at the time. But when you think about it. . . Wouldn't it have made more sense for him to keep wearing the DSS choker in terminal Dogma as opposed to allowing Kaworu to wear it? I mean, if it what Kaworu said is true, that he is truly guilty of genocide and Gaiacide, that his friends and family are either dead or will never forgive him for failing, that he is a pariah hated by the whole of humanity along with his father, that Kaworu ' s plan is a win-all/lose all scenario. Then why not keep the choker? If it fails he literally has nothing left to live for, and it won't matter if he lives or dies, and if it works the choker won't go off and he will be forgiven! Not to mention I think Kaworu living and sharing his knowledge of Seele and Nerv would a better benefit to WILLE's plan for a final battle with Gendo than a catatonic, borderline suicidal Shinji. Well you know what they say, hindsight is 20x20. I guess I'm just upset. I hope Final gives us a happy ending, because quite frankly after all Shinji's been through, all hes guilty of, ( whether of his own will or his fathers manipulation), maybe a quick merciful death would've been the best option. Again I'm being pessimistic, and I apologize for it. I want to be constructive, I don't want to be that kind of fan. I want to begin debate. What do you guys think? I'm going to catch a lot of flak for this one. . .I do not repeat, Do NOT want Shinji to die!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Apr 04, 2014 2:42 am Shinji can't trigger the DSS Choker himself, so keeping it to quickly kill himself if the plan fails is completely stupid. Besides, I have the feeling that the DSS Choker would had activated no matter what spears were in Lilith's Chamber : Kaworu's plan was to use the LoL and LoC to "repair the world", meaning -if he was straightforward in his explanation- to basically terraform the whole planet. And the only beings we saw being able to perform such a feat are Seeds of Life (Liliths and ADAMs) or an awakened Evangelion. And Kaworu knew too much of what happened in Lilith's Chamber to ignore that the spear didn't killed the 12th Angel but froze it. So for me in Kaworu's plan, the events were supposed to happen exactly as how it happened in Q, liberation and absorption of the Angel, awakening and Impact included, except that EVA-13 was to wield a LoL and a LoC instead of two LoL, which would had somehow changed to outcome (how much, that's a mystery) So of course the DSS Choker would had activated too, and I think that's why Kaworu took it from Shinji : because unlike him, Kaworu as powers to stop the DSS Choker from detonating. So in the end, if everything goes as planned, the world is repaired and Kaworu keep the DSS Choker in check, and if something goes wrong, Kaworu can stale the DSS from detonating long enough to give some parting words with Shinji and then dying to stop the Impact and protect Shinji, which is what happened in 3.0. And probably how Gendo counted that Kaworu would act.

[]:             Quote Post by xyz346 » Fri Apr 04, 2014 8:23 pm Actually, if Shinji kept wearing the collar, he would have likely never piloted EVA-13 to begin with. It took Kaworu taking the collar to get him to even considering piloting.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sat Apr 05, 2014 5:07 am ElMariachi wrote: Shinji can't trigger the DSS Choker himself, so keeping it to quickly kill himself if the plan fails is completely stupid. Besides, I have the feeling that the DSS Choker would had activated no matter what spears were in Lilith's Chamber : Kaworu's plan was to use the LoL and LoC to "repair the world", meaning -if he was straightforward in his explanation- to basically terraform the whole planet. And the only beings we saw being able to perform such a feat are Seeds of Life (Liliths and ADAMs) or an awakened Evangelion. And Kaworu knew too much of what happened in Lilith's Chamber to ignore that the spear didn't killed the 12th Angel but froze it. So for me in Kaworu's plan, the events were supposed to happen exactly as how it happened in Q, liberation and absorption of the Angel, awakening and Impact included, except that EVA-13 was to wield a LoL and a LoC instead of two LoL, which would had somehow changed to outcome (how much, that's a mystery) So of course the DSS Choker would had activated too, and I think that's why Kaworu took it from Shinji : because unlike him, Kaworu as powers to stop the DSS Choker from detonating. So in the end, if everything goes as planned, the world is repaired and Kaworu keep the DSS Choker in check, and if something goes wrong, Kaworu can stale the DSS from detonating long enough to give some parting words with Shinji and dying to stop the Impact and protect Shinji, which is what happened in 3.0. And probably how Gendo counted that Kaworu would act.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Apr 05, 2014 6:15 am airman4 wrote: Kaworu could have left the DSS on the bed

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sat Apr 05, 2014 12:18 pm Monk Ed wrote: How many times must it be explained why Kaworu put on the DSS choker instead of chucking it away? Why, why, why must it constantly be re-explained? When the answer is already given in the movie itself when he puts it on ?! Whyyy? WHYYY?! In other words, Kaworu put on the DSS choker so that if someone has to die in order to avoid the (true) end of the world, it would be him. And in the end, it served exactly that purpose : It was Kaworu's death that allowed Unit 13 to be grabbed and its remaining pilot ejected, halting 4th Impact. If Kaworu had left the DSS choker on the bed, there would be no Final because everyone would be dead (or ... something. We are at least assured that a complete 4th Impact would have been very bad news).

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Apr 05, 2014 1:41 pm airman4 wrote: What do you mean by "or something" ? Do you imply another form of destruction of the humanity ? Lilins returning to lilith ?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 05, 2014 2:36 pm ElMariachi wrote: Shinji can't trigger the DSS Choker himself, so keeping it to quickly kill himself if the plan fails is completely stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sat Apr 05, 2014 3:10 pm Thanks guys for the explanation I get way better now this part of the film I need to see Q again It's way better with those infos . That is a price Gendo is willing to pay. Shinji's been through with him for a while at this point, if Bardiel didn't do it, the ruined city did. He was actively working to betray NeoNerv and thwart their impact plans at this point, how much worse would it get? NTI killed Hikari and the stooges, and got Rei killed, or worse, trapped in an "I must scream" scenario. How much worse does manipulating Shinji into offing one more best friend/love interest really make it? If the events of Q's last act prove anything, then it's that Gendo does not require Shinji's trust to make him do his dirty work.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Apr 05, 2014 3:37 pm Kendrix wrote: Even if he could, he wouldn't be capable of pulling the trigger, no matter how much he'd regret it afterwards, and you'd think he'd know at least this much about his limitations by now.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 05, 2014 3:48 pm airman4 wrote: His best element got his head blown off

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Apr 05, 2014 4:01 pm Kendrix wrote: I'd barely call someone who officially listens to SEELE and actually wants to doublecross them both for the sake of Lillimkind in its current form Gendo's "best Element" - his last talk with Fuyu does in fact suggest that Gendo needs Kaworu dead for his plan to work. So getting him dead, having tricked him to do it by his own hand no less, can be considered a clear sucess. SEELE probably would have preferred for this impact to go through.

Not to mention Kaworu did it to earn his trust and the poor kid was so racked with guilt and sadness and insomnia he couldn't exactly reason or think it through properly at the time.

[]: Should Shinji Have Kept The DSS Choker?             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Apr 04, 2014 2:24 am Okay. Obviously not! Anno wouldn't do that! He's the main character so until Final, the narrative is obviously going to favor what keeps him alive. Not to mention Kaworu did it to earn his trust and the poor kid was so racked with guilt and sadness and insomnia he couldn't exactly reason or think it through properly at the time. But when you think about it. . . Wouldn't it have made more sense for him to keep wearing the DSS choker in terminal Dogma as opposed to allowing Kaworu to wear it? I mean, if it what Kaworu said is true, that he is truly guilty of genocide and Gaiacide, that his friends and family are either dead or will never forgive him for failing, that he is a pariah hated by the whole of humanity along with his father, that Kaworu ' s plan is a win-all/lose all scenario. Then why not keep the choker? If it fails he literally has nothing left to live for, and it won't matter if he lives or dies, and if it works the choker won't go off and he will be forgiven! Not to mention I think Kaworu living and sharing his knowledge of Seele and Nerv would a better benefit to WILLE's plan for a final battle with Gendo than a catatonic, borderline suicidal Shinji. Well you know what they say, hindsight is 20x20. I guess I'm just upset. I hope Final gives us a happy ending, because quite frankly after all Shinji's been through, all hes guilty of, ( whether of his own will or his fathers manipulation), maybe a quick merciful death would've been the best option. Again I'm being pessimistic, and I apologize for it. I want to be constructive, I don't want to be that kind of fan. I want to begin debate. What do you guys think? I'm going to catch a lot of flak for this one. . .I do not repeat, Do NOT want Shinji to die!
